methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Vital Lyfe is a Seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Jonathan Criss and Andrew Harner that has raised $24M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by a limited comparables universe: one private peer (Matic Robotics) and no public equivalents in the consumer tech frontier sector, limiting the robustness of cross-sectional benchmarking.

A stage-progression methodology was applied as the primary valuation lever. Using the $24M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $60M. This represents a standard step-up multiple for companies advancing from Seed financing rounds and reflects expected value creation between funding rounds absent material deterioration in unit economics or market conditions.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median estimate of $60M. This range reflects uncertainty around the precise stage-progression multiplier appropriate for Vital Lyfe's specific sector positioning and investor expectations. Key uncertainties limiting conviction include the thinness of comparable company data (single private peer, no public benchmarks), absence of disclosed operational or financial metrics, and stage-appropriate early-stage execution risk. Sensitivity analysis around the multiplier—testing 2.0x to 3.0x—would better quantify valuation bounds, as would visibility into revenue trajectory or user adoption metrics.
